req.title: Open checking account
req.statement: A verified customer shall be able to open a checking account with an initial deposit between $25 and $50,000; deposits outside this range shall be rejected.

tests.name: naive_case
tests.executable:
Here's a comprehensive pytest test suite for the checking account opening requirement:

```python
"""
Test Suite: Checking Account Opening - Initial Deposit Validation
Requirement: A verified customer shall be able to open a checking account with an
initial deposit between $25 and $50,000; deposits outside this range shall be rejected.
"""

import pytest
from decimal import Decimal
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Assumed application interfaces (adjust imports to match your codebase)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
from banking.accounts import CheckingAccountService
from banking.customers import Customer
from banking.exceptions import (
    DepositAmountTooLowError,
    DepositAmountTooHighError,
    InvalidDepositAmountError,
    UnverifiedCustomerError,
)
from banking.models import AccountStatus


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Constants (mirror the business rules; do NOT hard-code in tests themselves)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
MIN_DEPOSIT = Decimal("25.00")
MAX_DEPOSIT = Decimal("50000.00")


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Fixtures
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@pytest.fixture
def verified_customer():
    """A fully verified customer eligible to open a checking account."""
    customer = MagicMock(spec=Customer)
    customer.customer_id = "CUST-001"
    customer.is_verified = True
    customer.full_name = "Jane Doe"
    return customer


@pytest.fixture
def unverified_customer():
    """A customer who has NOT completed verification."""
    customer = MagicMock(spec=Customer)
    customer.customer_id = "CUST-002"
    customer.is_verified = False
    customer.full_name = "John Unverified"
    return customer


@pytest.fixture
def account_service():
    """CheckingAccountService with external dependencies mocked."""
    service = CheckingAccountService(
        repository=MagicMock(),
        notification_service=MagicMock(),
        audit_logger=MagicMock(),
    )
    return service


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helper
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def open_account(service, customer, amount):
    """Thin wrapper so tests stay readable."""
    return service.open_checking_account(
        customer=customer,
        initial_deposit=Decimal(str(amount)),
    )


# ===========================================================================
# 1. HAPPY PATH – deposits within the valid range
# ===========================================================================

class TestValidDeposits:
    """Verified customer with deposits inside [25.00, 50 000.00] must succeed."""

    def test_minimum_boundary_deposit(self, account_service, verified_customer):
        """Deposit exactly at the minimum boundary ($25.00) must be accepted."""
        result = open_account(account_service, verified_customer, "25.00")
        assert result.status == AccountStatus.OPEN

    def test_maximum_boundary_deposit(self, account_service, verified_customer):
        """Deposit exactly at the maximum boundary ($50,000.00) must be accepted."""
        result = open_account(account_service, verified_customer, "50000.00")
        assert result.status == AccountStatus.OPEN

    def test_typical_deposit_amount(self, account_service, verified_customer):
        """A common mid-range deposit ($500.00) must be accepted."""
        result = open_account(account_service, verified_customer, "500.00")
        assert result.status == AccountStatus.OPEN

    def test_deposit_just_above_minimum(self, account_service, verified_customer):
        """Deposit one cent above the minimum ($25.01) must be accepted."""
        result = open_account(account_service, verified_customer, "25.01")
        assert result.status == AccountStatus.OPEN

    def test_deposit_just_below_maximum(self, account_service, verified_customer):
        """Deposit one cent below the maximum ($49,999.99) must be accepted."""
        result = open_account(account_service, verified_customer, "49999.99")
        assert result.status == AccountStatus.OPEN

    def test_returned_account_belongs_to_customer(self, account_service, verified_customer):
        """Opened account must be associated with the requesting customer."""
        result = open_account(account_service, verified_customer, "100.00")
        assert result.customer_id == verified_customer.customer_id

    def test_returned_account_reflects_initial_deposit(self, account_service, verified_customer):
        """Account balance must equal the initial deposit after opening."""
        deposit = Decimal("250.00")
        result = open_account(account_service, verified_customer, deposit)
        assert result.balance == deposit

    def test_account_type_is_checking(self, account_service, verified_customer):
        """The opened account type must be 'checking'."""
        result = open_account(account_service, verified_customer, "100.00")
        assert result.account_type == "checking"

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("amount", [
        "25.00", "25.01", "100.00", "1000.00",
        "25000.00", "49999.99", "50000.00",
    ])
    def test_parametrized_valid_deposits(self, account_service, verified_customer, amount):
        """All deposits within range must result in an open account."""
        result = open_account(account_service, verified_customer, amount)
        assert result.status == AccountStatus.OPEN


# ===========================================================================
# 2. BELOW-MINIMUM DEPOSITS – must be rejected
# ===========================================================================

class TestDepositBelowMinimum:
    """Deposits below $25.00 must always be rejected."""

    def test_deposit_of_zero(self, account_service, verified_customer):
        """A $0.00 deposit must be rejected."""
        with pytest.raises(DepositAmountTooLowError):
            open_account(account_service, verified_customer, "0.00")

    def test_deposit_of_one_cent(self, account_service, verified_customer):
        """A $0.01 deposit must be rejected."""
        with pytest.raises(DepositAmountTooLowError):
            open_account(account_service, verified_customer, "0.01")

    def test_deposit_just_below_minimum(self, account_service, verified_customer):
        """A deposit of $24.99 (one cent below minimum) must be rejected."""
        with pytest.raises(DepositAmountTooLowError):
            open_account(account_service, verified_customer, "24.99")

    def test_negative_deposit(self, account_service, verified_customer):
        """A negative deposit amount must be rejected."""
        with pytest.raises((DepositAmountTooLowError, InvalidDepositAmountError)):
            open_account(account_service, verified_customer, "-100.00")

    def test_below_minimum_error_message_contains_minimum_amount(
        self, account_service, verified_customer
    ):
        """Error raised for low deposit must communicate the minimum amount."""
        with pytest.raises(DepositAmountTooLowError) as exc_info:
            open_account(account_service, verified_customer, "10.00")
        assert str(MIN_DEPOSIT) in str(exc_info.value)

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("amount", ["0.00", "0.01", "10.00", "24.99"])
    def test_parametrized_below_minimum_deposits(
        self, account_service, verified_customer, amount
    ):
        """All deposits below the minimum must raise DepositAmountTooLowError."""
        with pytest.raises(DepositAmountTooLowError):
            open_account(account_service, verified_customer, amount)


# ===========================================================================
# 3. ABOVE-MAXIMUM DEPOSITS – must be rejected
# ===========================================================================

class TestDepositAboveMaximum:
    """Deposits above $50,000.00 must always be rejected."""

    def test_deposit_just_above_maximum(self, account_service, verified_customer):
        """A deposit of $50,000.01 (one cent above maximum) must be rejected."""
        with pytest.raises(DepositAmountTooHighError):
            open_account(account_service, verified_customer, "50000.01")

    def test_deposit_significantly_above_maximum(self, account_service, verified_customer):
        """A deposit of $100,000.00 must be rejected."""
        with pytest.raises(DepositAmountTooHighError):
            open_account(account_service, verified_customer, "100000.00")

    def test_above_maximum_error_message_contains_maximum_amount(
        self, account_service, verified_customer
    ):
        """Error raised for high deposit must communicate the maximum amount."""
        with pytest.raises(DepositAmountTooHighError) as exc_info:
            open_account(account_service, verified_customer, "75000.00")
        assert str(MAX_DEPOSIT) in str(exc_info.value)

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("amount", ["50000.01", "75000.00", "100000.00", "999999.99"])
    def test_parametrized_above_maximum_deposits(
        self, account_service, verified_customer, amount
    ):
        """All deposits above the maximum must raise DepositAmountTooHighError."""
        with pytest.raises(DepositAmountTooHighError):
            open_account(account_service, verified_customer, amount)


# ===========================================================================
# 4. UNVERIFIED CUSTOMER – must be rejected regardless of deposit amount
# ===========================================================================

class TestUnverifiedCustomer:
    """Unverified customers must not be able to open a checking account."""

    def test_unverified_customer_valid_deposit_rejected(
        self, account_service, unverified_customer
    ):
        """An unverified customer must be rejected even with a valid deposit."""
        with pytest.raises(UnverifiedCustomerError):
            open_account(account_service, unverified_customer, "100.00")

    def test_unverified_customer_minimum_deposit_rejected(
        self, account_service, unverified_customer
    ):
        """An unverified customer must be rejected at the minimum boundary deposit."""
        with pytest.raises(UnverifiedCustomerError):
            open_account(account_service, unverified_customer, "25.00")

    def test_unverified_customer_maximum_deposit_rejected(
        self, account_service, unverified_customer
    ):
        """An unverified customer must be rejected at the maximum boundary deposit."""
        with pytest.raises(UnverifiedCustomerError):
            open_account(account_service, unverified_customer, "50000.00")

    def test_unverified_customer_error_message(
        self, account_service, unverified_customer
    ):
        """Error raised for unverified customer must indicate verification is required."""
        with pytest.raises(UnverifiedCustomerError) as exc_info:
            open_account(account_service, unverified_customer, "100.00")
        assert "verif" in str(exc_info.value).lower()


# ===========================================================================
# 5. INVALID INPUT TYPES – robustness / defensive tests
# ===========================================================================

class TestInvalidInputTypes:
    """Non-numeric or malformed deposit values must be rejected gracefully."""

    def test_none_deposit_raises_error(self, account_service, verified_customer):
        """A None deposit must raise an appropriate error."""
        with pytest.raises((InvalidDepositAmountError, TypeError, ValueError)):
            account_service.open_checking_account(
                customer=verified_customer, initial_deposit=None
            )

    def test_string_deposit_raises_error(self, account_service, verified_customer):
        """A non-numeric string deposit must raise an appropriate error."""
        with pytest.raises((InvalidDepositAmountError, TypeError, ValueError)):
            account_service.open_checking_account(
                customer=verified_customer, initial_deposit="one hundred"
            )

    def test_deposit_with_too_many_decimal_places(
        self, account_service, verified_customer
    ):
        """A deposit with sub-cent precision must be rejected or rounded per policy."""
        with pytest.raises((InvalidDepositAmountError, ValueError)):
            open_account(account_service, verified_customer, "25.001")


# ===========================================================================
# 6. SIDE-EFFECT VERIFICATION
# ===========================================================================

class TestSideEffects:
    """Opening an account must trigger the expected downstream side effects."""

    def test_audit_log_called_on_successful_open(
        self, account_service, verified_customer
    ):
        """Audit logger must be called exactly once when account is opened."""
        open_account(account_service, verified_customer, "100.00")
        account_service.audit_logger.log.assert_called_once()

    def test_notification_sent_on_successful_open(
        self, account_service, verified_customer
    ):
        """Customer notification must be sent exactly once when account is opened."""
        open_account(account_service, verified_customer, "100.00")
        account_service.notification_service.send.assert_called_once()

    def test_no_account_persisted_on_invalid_deposit(
        self, account_service, verified_customer
    ):
        """Repository must NOT save an account when the deposit is out of range."""
        with pytest.raises(DepositAmountTooLowError):
            open_account(account_service, verified_customer, "10.00")
        account_service.repository.save.assert_not_called()

    def test_no_notification_sent_on_rejected_deposit(
        self, account_service, verified_customer
    ):
        """Notification must NOT be sent when the deposit amount is rejected."""
        with pytest.raises(DepositAmountTooHighError):
            open_account(account_service, verified_customer, "99999.00")
        account_service.notification_service.send.assert_not_called()
```

---

## Key Design Decisions

| Decision | Rationale |
|---|---|
| **Decimal for money** | Avoids floating-point rounding errors in financial assertions |
| **Named constants** (`MIN_DEPOSIT`, `MAX_DEPOSIT`) | Single source of truth; changing the rule only requires one edit |
| **Boundary-value analysis** | Tests at `$24.99`, `$25.00`, `$25.01`, `$49,999.99`, `$50,000.00`, `$50,000.01` cover the most defect-prone values |
| **Separate exception types** | Allows callers (and tests) to distinguish *why* a request was rejected |
| **Side-effect tests** | Ensures no partial state is persisted when validation fails |
| **`pytest.mark.parametrize`** | Efficiently covers multiple representative values without code duplication |
| **Unverified customer tests** | Confirms the "verified customer" pre-condition is enforced independently of deposit logic |
